title: Fixing Node Duplication in a Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Given a binary tree with node values, insert a new node into the tree without overwriting any existing nodes. However, the current code overwrites duplicates without handling them correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The binary tree is represented as a root node, and the new node to be inserted is provided as a string of its value.
statement.outputFormat: The constructed binary tree should be returned. If any values are overwritten (due to duplicates), handle this situation.
statement.notes: Use a binary tree node representation with a value, and left and right child pointers.

examples.input: Example input (matching the stdin format that main wrapper expects)

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: TreeNode* insertIntoTree(TreeNode* root, int value) { // TODO: implement };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public TreeNode insertIntoTree(TreeNode root, int value) { // TODO: implement };
starterCode.python: class Solution: def insertIntoTree(self, root: TreeNode, value: int) -> TreeNode: # TODO: implement;
starterCode.javascript: function insertIntoTree(root, value) { // TODO: implement; return root; }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: TreeNode* insertIntoTree(TreeNode* root, int value) { if (root == nullptr) { return new TreeNode(value); } else if (value == root->value) { return root; } else { root->left = new TreeNode(value); return root; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public TreeNode insertIntoTree(TreeNode root, int value) { if (root == null) { return new TreeNode(value); } else if (value == root.value) { return root; } else { root.left = new TreeNode(value); return root; } };
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def insertIntoTree(self, root: TreeNode, value: int) -> TreeNode: if not root: return TreeNode(value); elif value == root.val: return root; else: root.left = TreeNode(value); return root;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function insertIntoTree(root, value) { if (!root) { return { value: value, left: null, right: null }; } else if (value === root.value) { return root; } else { root.left = { value: value, left: null, right: null }; return root; } }

functionSignature.python: def insertIntoTree(self, root: Optional[TreeNode], value: int) -> Optional[TreeNode]:

solutions.python: class Solution: def insertIntoTree(self, root: Optional[TreeNode], value: int) -> Optional[TreeNode]: if not root: return TreeNode(value); node = TreeNode(value); if value < root.val: root.left = self.insertIntoTree(root.left, value); else: root.right = self.insertIntoTree(root.right, value); return root;
solutions.cpp: #include <iostream> using namespace std; class Solution { TreeNode* insertIntoTree(TreeNode* root, int value) { if (root == nullptr) { return new TreeNode(value); } else if (value < root->value) { root->left = insertIntoTree(root->left, value); } else { root->right = insertIntoTree(root->right, value); } return root; }; };
